title: ranunculus ficaria f. divergens
intro: Ranunculus ficaria f. divergens, also known as lesser celandine, is a perennial plant with shiny, yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves, often found growing near streams and waterways. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of lesser celandine include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy. Lesser celandine can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote healing and well-being.
